title: -Companion Report: City Compliance with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG)

sections.recommendation:
The City Manager recommends that Council refrain from referring the Commission on
Disability’s report recommendation from the report titled, “City Compliance with Web
Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG),” to the City Attorney's Office at this time to
give staff and the Commission on Disability additional time to develop a mutually
agreeable path forward.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for staff time for the administration of the first recommendation has not yet
been calculated within each City department. The addition contracts clause requiring
vendors supply WCAG-compliant digital content would involve the following costs,
which are not included in the current City budget:
 City Attorney’s Office staff time and/or a contractor to develop the contract
language,
 Finance Department staff time to update contract boilerplates,
 Additional staff training on steps to correct simple accessibility errors,
 Potential increase in contractor costs to adhere to accessibility requirements,
 Staff time from every City department, as well as Library, Rent Board, and
Housing Authority to monitor contracts and ensure compliance
Costs to implement the second recommendation, to put in place systems ensuring
documents meet WCAG standards, have not been estimated but are likely to be
significant given then number of documents currently on the city website
While the City’s website was designed to be WCAG compliant, the City recognizes that
the constant publication of non-compliant documents creates substantial barriers for
community members and the City’s own ability to maintain a WCAG compliant site.
Publications should be WCAG compliant upon web publication. The recommendation to
remediate all current website documents within 30 days of being informed they do not

sections.current_situation:
Accessibility is a core principle that informed the design and build of berkeleyca.gov,
which launched May 2022. The City’s website software, design and architecture were
built to WCAG standards and continue to be updated to improve accessibility.
Nearly 100 staff across the City of Berkeley, Rent Board, and Housing Authority have
access to add content to the City website. Accessibility is woven in to all the web
training these staff receive. The Communications team in the City Manager’s Office has
led three trainings in the past year specifically addressing WCAG document
accessibility and created a two page guide on best practices.
The City’s ADA Program Coordinator currently provides support for WCAG compliance
on a case-by-case basis and will continue to do so.
Despite the City’s ongoing work to support full WCAG compliance, many PDF
documents are added to the website on a daily basis, and PDFs are a challenging
format to make accessible. Many are created by 3rd-party legacy systems managed by
different City Departments. With over 16,000 documents on berkeleyca.gov and dozens
added each day, document accessibility is a large, citywide issue. In addition to the
aforementioned training, staff prioritize remediating upon public request and converting
to HTML the most commonly accessed documents, such as recreation schedules and
City fees and rates.
The City Manager supports the adoption of a contractual requirement for all vendors to
be WCAG compliant and is committed to supporting staff’s work with a joint staff/
Commission on Disability Commissioner taskforce to address all of the commission’s
concerns as collaboratively as possible.
sections.background:
The Commission on Disability took the following action at its April 9, 2025 meeting:
Action: Motion (Fischer/Lewkowicz) To approve the recommendation to City Council
regarding WCAG.
Votes: Ayes: Walsh, Fischer, Pugh, Sun, Lewkowicz, Mann, and Holloway; Nays: None;
Abstain: None; Unexcused Absence: None; Leave of Absence: Paz
On July 9, 2025, the Commission on Disability received a staff presentation regarding
the status of existing compliance efforts and resource challenges related to
implementation. Based on staff’s feedback, the Commission voted to create a
subcommittee that would work directly with staff to establish a WCAG Task Force that
Page 3 of 3
Companion Report: City Compliance with Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) CONSENT CALENDAR
July 29, 2025
would be tasked to collaboratively develop a roadmap to identify WCAG priorities and
implementation phasing timelines.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
None have been identified at this time.
sections.rationale:
It is necessary to understand the full budgetary implication of remediation and ongoing
compliance with WCAG, prior to adoption of the Commission’s Recommendation.
sections.alternatives:
Beginning to immediately require WCAG compliance requires staff to begin planning to
remove or update an unknown number of existing documents within the recommended
30-day period. An assessment of existing documents out of WCAG compliance has not
been conducted in every City department. The City currently does not have the required
budget or staff technical expertise for complete compliance. An outside vendor to
conduct this assessment is not part of an existing budget.
CONTACT PERSON
